content.problemStatement:
The median of a sequence is the element in the middle of the sequence after it is sorted. For a sequence with even size, the median is the average of the two middle elements of the sequence after sorting. For example, for a sequence A=[1,3,3,5,4,7,11]A = [1, 3, 3, 5, 4, 7, 11]A=[1,3,3,5,4,7,11], the median is equal to 444, and for A=[2,3,4,5]A = [2, 3, 4, 5]A=[2,3,4,5], the median is equal to (3+4)/2=3.5(3+4)/2 = 3.5(3+4)/2=3.5.

Fulu is a famous programmer in his country. He wrote the following program (given in pseudocode) for finding the median in a sequence AAA with length NNN:

`read(N)
read(A[0], A[1], ..., A[N-1])
sort(A)
# simultaneously remove elements A[K] and A[N mod K] from the array A
# the order of the remaining N-2 elements is unchanged
remove(A[K], A[N mod K])
return A[(N-2)/2] # integer division
`The program takes an integer KKK as a parameter. Fulu can choose this integer arbitrarily between 111 and N−1N-1N−1 inclusive.

Little Lima, Fulu's friend, thinks Fulu's program is wrong (as always). As finding one counterexample would be an easy task for Lima (he has already found the sequence A=[34,23,35,514]A = [34, 23, 35, 514]A=[34,23,35,514], which is a counterexample for any K≤3K \le 3K≤3), Lima decided to make hacking more interesting. Fulu should give him four parameters S,K,m,MS, K, m, MS,K,m,M (in addition to NNN) and Lima should find the lexicographically smallest proper sequence AAA with length NNN as a counterexample.

We say that a sequence AAA with length NNN (000-indexed) is proper if it satisfies the following conditions:


it contains only positive integers
A0+A1+A2+⋯+AN−1=SA_0 + A_1 +A_2 + \dots + A_{N-1} = SA0​+A1​+A2​+⋯+AN−1​=S
m≤Ai≤Mm \le A_i \le Mm≤Ai​≤M for each 0≤i<N0 \le i \lt N0≤i<N
the number returned by Fulu's program, ran with the given parameter KKK, is different from the correct median of the sequence AAA
Can you help Lima find the lexicographically smallest counterexample or determine that Fulu's program works perfectly for the given parameters?
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains five space-separated integers NNN, SSS, KKK, mmm and MMM.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, if there is no proper sequence, print a single line containing the integer −1-1−1; otherwise, print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers — the lexicographically smallest proper sequence.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: For the sequence A=[1,1,4]A = [1, 1, 4]A=[1,1,4], it is already sorted, so the program would just remove the first two elements (K=1K=1K=1, N%K=0N\%K=0N%K=0) and return the only remaining element 444. Obviously, the median of the original sequence is 111. It can be shown that there is no lexicographically smaller proper sequence.
Example case 2: The only possible sequence is A=[1,1,1,1]A = [1, 1, 1, 1]A=[1,1,1,1], which is not proper, since Fulu's program will give the correct answer 111 for it.
